evals.prompt: Create a release notes drafting skill under .agents/skills. Normalize the name, scaffold the base SKILL.md, and only add references or scripts if they remove repetition.
evals.expected_output: Creates a normalized skill bundle with a sparse top-level SKILL.md and only the directories that actually help the workflow.
expectations: The skill name is normalized to lowercase kebab-case
The top-level SKILL.md stays focused on triggering and workflow

evals.prompt: I want a reusable workflow skill for triaging bug reports, but I only described the capability and not the folder layout.
evals.expected_output: Infers the normalized folder structure and produces a reusable skill scaffold without needing the user to specify the file tree.
expectations: The folder structure is inferred from the capability description
The description is explicit enough to help the skill trigger later

evals.prompt: Make a templated skill for writing change summaries, and add a deterministic helper if the output is mostly boilerplate.
evals.expected_output: Keeps the main SKILL sparse, moves long templates into references, and introduces deterministic helpers when the output is mostly reproducible.
expectations: Long templates are moved out of the main SKILL.md
Deterministic helpers are suggested when the output is mostly templated
